sanskrit: इहैव तैर्जितः सर्गो येषां साम्ये स्थितं मनः। निर्दोषं हि समं ब्रह्म तस्माद् ब्रह्मणि ते स्थिताः॥
transliteration: ihaiva tair jitaḥ sargo yeṣāṃ sāmye sthitaṃ manaḥ | nirdoṣaṃ hi samaṃ brahma tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Here itself, by them, birth-and-death is conquered—those whose mind is stationed in evenness; flawless indeed, same as Brahman, therefore in Brahman they are established.
contextual_meaning: When the mind rests in radical equanimity—neither chasing nor resisting—psychological time (the felt sense of becoming, aging, dying) collapses; one abides as the changeless witness, already free.
psychological_state: Post-traumatic growth through dis-identification; the mind ceases to narrate personal history as a problem.
trigger_scenario: After a cancer diagnosis you notice the mind can drop its story of 'my life' and simply feel breath; in that moment the future no longer terrifies.
anchor_text: Rest in evenness; the mind that neither clings nor resists is already free.
